operating_state: Total net sales decreased 5% to $117.2 billion driven by foreign currency weakness across all geographic segments. iPhone sales declined 8% from lower new model sales, Mac fell 29% primarily from reduced MacBook Pro demand, while iPad increased 30% and Services grew 6% from cloud services and App Store expansion. COVID-related disruptions temporarily reduced iPhone 14 Pro assembly capacity at the primary Zhengzhou facility.
cost_structure: Total gross margin percentage declined to 43.0% from 43.8% primarily due to foreign currency weakness and lower product volumes. Products gross margin decreased to 37.0% from 38.4% driven by currency headwinds, while Services gross margin fell to 70.8% from 72.4% due to currency impacts and higher service costs. Operating expenses increased to 12% of sales from 10% driven by higher headcount-related expenses across R&D and SG&A functions.
material_events: The company repurchased $19.0 billion of common stock and paid $3.8 billion in dividends during the quarter. Apple launched new iPad and iPad Pro powered by M2 chip alongside Apple TV 4K products. The company maintained its quarterly dividend at $0.23 per share with intent for annual increases subject to board approval.
macro_exposures: Foreign currency weakness relative to the U.S. dollar significantly impacted sales across all segments and compressed gross margins. Macroeconomic conditions including inflation, rising interest rates, and currency fluctuations continue affecting business operations directly and indirectly. COVID-related disruptions at key Chinese manufacturing facilities created supply constraints for premium iPhone models.
forward_outlook: Management expects gross margins to remain subject to volatility and downward pressure from various operational factors. The company maintains confidence in cash generation from operations and debt market access to satisfy capital requirements and return programs over the next 12 months. Manufacturing purchase obligations totaled $55.1 billion with substantially all payable within 12 months covering demand periods up to 150 days.
uncertainty_profile: No material changes to risk factors or critical accounting estimates were reported since the prior annual filing. The company maintains commercial paper outstanding payable within 12 months. Ongoing COVID-19 pandemic impacts on supply chain partners and component suppliers remain a source of operational uncertainty, particularly affecting Chinese manufacturing facilities and assembly operations.
